free_text: Last two authors
Fission neutron spectrum averaged cross sections of
some threshold reactions on molybdenum: estimation of
radioactive impurities in (n,gamma)-produced Mo-99 -
Tc-99-m generator system
Subents 013-015
5 MW swimming pool type
neutron flux of 6.7 x 10**12 n/cm2/s
From the fact that the K-factor (ratio fast- and therm.
flux density), using monitor reactions with different
thresholds, was almost constant (within 10%), it was
concluded that the neutron spectrum used was a fission
spectrum.
Mo-oxide (purity 99.999%) or enriched Mo-92-oxide,
and enriched Ni-58 powder filled in quartz ampoules
and then placed in an NRX type irradiation capsule
Radiochemical separation of niobium and
zirconium from the matrix activity, which contained
large quantities of Mo-99 and Tc-99-m, and of cobalt
and nickel from the irradiated Ni-58.
For measuring the X-rays emitted in the decay
of Nb-91-m a Si(Li) detector was used.
Each cross section value is a mean of the
results of at least six independent measurements
From these four monitor threshold reactions the fast
neutron flux densities, effective at the ampoules,
were determined.
The thermal flux, effective at the ampoules, was
determined from these two reactions
The count rates were converted to decay rates by
applying corrections for radiochemical yields,
gamma-ray intensities, detector efficiencies, counting
geometry, absorption, self-absorption, pile-up effects,
coincidence losses, and contributions from decay of
parents
Reported errors incorporate both statistical
and systematical errors
Main errors were those associated with determination
of:
Fast neutron flux densities (8%) and
absolute decay rates (5 to 10%)
Other sources of errors were < 1%
